reviews.review_text: Its a good coop game, but you cant make it work on modern machines without disabling e-cores.

Doesn't run on modern computers using Intel chips with P and E cores. I tried everything but the game sits on my last two efficiency cores. Shame as it looked like a very nice game, regardless of sale to play with my friends, i even bought them copies while it was on sale. Shame i will need to refund it.

Ubisoft response: Just go into BIOS everytime you want to play and disable your E cores.

This is why Ubisoft is trash, they just dont care.

! ! ! READ TO FIX INFINITE LOADING SCREEN ! ! !

go into your bios, disable "E cores". i just saved you 5 hours. 

ubisoft suck my ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ♥♥♥♥.

great game!
8.5/10 for me. It's a massive open world game that isn't doing anything crazy, but for me it's one of my favorite comfort games.

So to preface I will say; I do really like this this game. I remember 100% completing it on Ps4 way back in 2017 when it first came out, and it was such a blast. Now, 9 years later, playing it again is just as great as it was back then. Wildlands is 1000% the go-to for modern Ghost Recon games, and is definitely a lot better than Breakpoint (with no always-online requirement, so that alone wins out). The map, the story, the atmosphere, the visuals - all chef's kiss.

There's only one major issue (and the reason for my thumbs down review): the game does not run on modern intel processors. This is because newer processors have hybid efficiency cores (E-cores) and the version of Easy Anti Cheat the game is shipped with is not compatible with E-cores. Once you launch the game you are met with an infinite loading screen that never actually loads. Many other users also have this issue, and Ubisoft themselves have acklowledged it - yet stated that no fix is planned.

The only way to actually play this game involves either going into BIOS and disabling E-cores + Hyperthreading (NOT RECOMMENDED), or by using the EAC Bypass mod (found via Steam Guides > Modding or Config > "Fixing Ghost Recon Infinite Loading Screen"). The latter is how I managed to get the game to work, but it only works for singleplayer (can't play online without EAC running).

So basically, if you do have a newer processor then maybe think twice about purchasing this game. Whilst the game itself is probably my favourite GR game of the series (even beating out Vegas 2), it's a complete joke that Ubisoft, a company with multiple hundreds of employees, can't assign 1 small team for like a week to actually make this game playable. Nope. Spent your hard-earnt money on our product that doesn't even work? Too bad - but we'll still take your money anyway....

Speaking of Ubislop, there is also now a 'lootbox' system that has been tacked on years after launch, and it SUCKS. Most cosmetics are now locked behind RNG-based lootboxes, with the only method of aquiring them being through ludicrously priced in-game currency, or ultra slow/tedious daily challenge grind. And at the end of all of it, you're STILL bound by RNG. Classic moneygrubbing Ubisoft. Ugh.

It doesn't feel like a ghost recon game but rather a game like farcry your with a crew but its basically like a open world territorial game where you clear people out, and gather weapons so from this perspective i did not find it as enjoyable or reward worthy as its sort of just a repetitive npcs, and sort of that difficulty open world typical view where you enter a zone thats harder or easier as the game goes on.

How is the concept of the game? From an overall view i like the aspect they were going for but i dislike how poor they made the entire territorial system as it feels like your just doing a repeat with no real gain in anything you do your always going to be with your squad and the back up system feels poor there's a ton of cars whether they be sports to like a shooter car but poorly displayed as a way of just grabbing what you want without reason

What would i rate this game? From an overall view i would give it a valid 6/10 its decent but its repetitive its something you would put at least 1-2 hours max and you'd get bored of the repetitiveness

Would i recommend it overall? If you had a poor amount of games its not the worst but from an aspect you would want and expect better so Yes from this view but no at full price defo worth $10-5 Its like a farcry game but when you clear an area you dont get it, or nobody take it as a team you just move onto the next so from this view id say farcry is better

Alright, I can't lie, it's not a great game, it's an ok game in my opinion.

The first few hours of the game are fun, lots of unique interactions and missions feeling like you're actually taking down the big bad guys. Makes sense when doing a video game to hook people to play your game, but afterwards the quality of missions goes down and they just feel numb. 

Lots of bugs plague the missions and even the achievement. I usually go to 100% an achievement when I play a game, but the progress ones like "Mission Master", the one with the medals and the one with collectibles didn't register my stuff properly, forcing me to restart a campaign. At least I've unlocked them halfway through but cmon, Some missions just bug halfway through, forcing you to abandon the mission or restart your game. (I won't lie tho, some of the bugs with friends are hilarious)

That being said, the game, at least when it was released, was fun. Cool little easter eggs and side missions. The weapon and caracter customization are great, quite a lot of stuff you can add. The DLCs are OK. I wasn't a big fan of the Narco Road, felt too goofy compared to the main game but Fallen Ghost was good and brought quite a lot of new enemy types.

In 2026, I would honestly rather buy Breakpoint, but I think Wildlands can still be fun to play with friends so I would get it on sales.

Feels like Far Cry Lite. 

It could have been an incredible game. World is beautiful. Shooting feels great when aiming down sights. The rest is really frustrating.

Moving around the world is tedious as vehicles are terrible to control (worst in any game I can think of). The AI sucks - your squad is slow and can (and will) ruin stealth, and if a single enemy spots you, every enemy spots you. Worst of all, it is a buggy mess. It semi-frequently crashes, and frequently has game breaking bugs (like immortal enemies, enemies teleporting close, missions not ending despite having done the objectives, missing characters, etc.

Can provide many hours of fun, IF you are patient as all hell, as the bugs, crashes, AI and vehicles really ruined a game which when working (almost half of the time) is very fun. To put into context, about every second mission was struck with one of these bugs, and every two to three hours it crashed.

So I, like many others, have been experiencing the failure to run issue. To be clear my issue is from steam hitting the green button to start the game, the button turns blue and says cancel, the button turns blue and says stop, and then turns green and says play all in a matter of about 3 seconds. 

I went through every recommended fix for the game from overlays, to the ubisoft connect app, uninstalling both the ubisoft connect app and game, all document files, reinstalling the recommended newest VC+ files etc. 

I did one thing that actually pointed me in the right direction. Go to your hardrive (C:) folder, Program Files (x86), Steam, steamapps, common, Wildlands. Scroll down to the GRW application file and right click. Click Run as an Administrator. Immediately i had an error that said "MSVCR110.DLL was not found. This pointed me to an older version of Visual Studio 2012 (VC++ 11.0) was not installed. 

I would guess that a major update to windows caused this older version of C++ 2012 to be removed.  I reinstalled and BAM it works again. Hope this helps someone. I am running an AMD CPU so this is not to be confused with the intel issues others have had.

I had it on a different platform before Steam and played 2-3 times through the campaign. To play it with m friends I got it on PC and I have to say it is still a good game in 2026. Obviously there is no raytracing, but overall the Graphics are still stunning and the map is gigantic. The only thing I dislike is the requirement for Uplay.

Atmosphere: 

This game is visually stunning. The environment, lighting, weather, and landscapes are all very amazing and make you feel like your in the world. The world also feels very 'lived in' as there are actually people walking around, not just in towns and cities, but also along the road. There are people driving around, so be careful when speeding around places. You can 'borrow' cars from people if needed and the car destruction is also very nice. Also, unlike in Breakpoint, if you lose your headlights, yo still keep them as there is just light sources coming from in front of the car. 

Story:

This game has an amazing story to go with its outstanding visuals. The story is cohesive, understandable, and generally more though out and executed better. I feel like I'm actually taking down a cartel, you can feel it weakening after your actions. This ties into the atmosphere, but throughout the world you can hear citizens and sicarios alike talking about your actions and how Buchons are going missing. All of the intel that you can collect throughout the world actually ties into the story, unlike Breakpoint where it tells the story of the ISLAND or leads you to 'treasures'. The information videos sent by Bowman are also very engaging, help with telling the story, and just look nice. Additionally, the optional collaboration missions
with Rainbow 6, other Ghosts, and Sam Fisher are also very enjoyable to play through.

Guns and Gunplay:

The guns are amazing in this game. They sound powerful when a suppressor isn't equipped, they look nice, they feel good to use. However, I do like how Breakpoint has a few more different weapons (Boss weapons and weapon designs don't count) and that there are many more attachments for your guns. A lot of the attachments in Breakpoint have the same stats yes, but I don't care. I want my guns to look cool and having a bunch of random attachments helps with that. I think encounters with enemies are also really good. They properly communicate and use tactics and don't act like complete idiots. I also like the different levels of enemies. A basic sicario patrol is much different then an Unidad patrol, it makes you think before rushing into a base.

Ghost Customization:

I think its a win for Breakpoint tbh. Wildlands has good customization, but Breakpoint has more, at least in terms of clothing. It is also much easier to get a lot of the clothing items as you can find most of it in the world or buy it from Maria's store. Of course some who have to get at the Ubisoft store, but you don't need to for most of it. As for body customization, its basically the same, I have no notes.

Miscellaneous:

* Menus are practical and easy to navigate
* Sound design is amazing; the music is awesome and its nice to hear people talking and vehicles humming
* The loading screens are imo, much cooler than those in Breakpoint
* It hasn't bee a buggy mess for me, game runs smooth as warm butter

I will note that you may have to change some settings to properly play. If you try and move around while ADS-ing and the camera shakes and you pop in and out of ADS, you will have to change a setting. You just have to make the game fullscreen, even if it already looks like it is. I can't recall what it is or where its located, but you can check google and find a Reddit thread.

TL;DR: Game looks good, plays good, has a good story, has decent customization and could be better, but still good, and make sure you're in FULLSCREEN. 9/10, amazing game.

The best word to describe this game is lazy. 

The mission design is unbelievably repetitive, every village/location looks the same, the characters are cardboard, the story is forgettable, vehicles feel awful to drive/pilot other than the chopper, stealth/tactics feel half-baked compared to even old ghost games, if you play solo your AI teammates are completely useless and can't figure out where to go, AI npcs you have to escort/protect are determined to get themselves killed, Unidad added nothing to the game other than being the equivalent of GTA infinite cop respawn, etc. etc. 

I feel like I could list 100 more annoying things about this game but this should give you enough of an idea. The whole game feels like some half-assed thrown together pile of ♥♥♥♥ on top of a generic battle royal map. I can't believe people were hyping this up as an underrated modern ghost recon game, breakpoint must be just that bad. The only positive I really have is some of the environments look nice but you'll get tired of that pretty quickly with how annoying and samey the rest of the game feels.

I don't even know what the point of the game saving during some of the missions is btw because if you fail you have to redo the whole thing anyway, thanks Ubisoft. Oh and whoever designed that Sam Fisher cameo mission should never be employed again. Oh the cool stealth op guy from splinter cell this should be fun, how about no, enjoy this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ room defense mission where you are swarmed by 100 guys shooting you from every angle while he ♥♥♥♥♥ with the computer and your AI teammates are eating glue.

Waste of everyone's time.
the game is fun but like half way thru the game one of the team members are stuck saying one line " Shall we return to are regularly-schedule program of liberating" wish there was a way it fix it.

I and many others who bought this on newer computers have had major issues attempting to get past an infinite loading screen glitch that Ubisoft does not seem to take seriously, giving only copy and paste advice for a game that is completely broken to many players. I have played this game for years prior on my old Xbox One when it released and while the game itself is great, this current state is completely unacceptable to those who bought it. Lastly, I highly recommend anyone who has had this issue to make a review and a support claim so that they hopefully (but unlikely) will fix this major issue.

[h1] ONLY BUY THIS GAME ON DISCOUNT, DON'T PAY FULL PRICE FOR THIS OLD GAME [/h1]

[h2]Having that said, god this game is fun.[/h2] This was basically my entrance to the "Tom Clancy'/Ghost Recon[i](?)[/i]'' saga and i enjoyed it so much.

Story is great, its not a bible perse, but its a great introduction for what it will happen throughout the whole playthrough. Althought for what i've heard, this was suppossed to be on Mexico instead of Bolivia, [b] AND IT SHOWS [/b] because some dialogues, enemies or even places look more ''mexico related'' than bolivian, but for obvious reasons that i don't think i need to share about my magical country of Mexico, this didn't happen and Ubisoft moved it to Bolivia. [i] (Im speaking mostly from ignorace so please don't take this part too seriously and you're welcome to correct me if im wrong on this [/i].
[h3] SPOILER FOR THE FINAL MISSION[/h3]
[spoiler] The final mission is the most pain in the ass i have ever experienced and i hope to ever replay that ♥♥♥♥ ever again. Literally hell to play when you have EVERYONE shoot at you and unless you have your vehicule upgrades maxxed out, its almost impossible to finish it because of the "betrayal" of the Bolivian rebellion [/spoiler]

The Game is good for what it offers, and i love the stealth mode althought it has its problems i love having to use the sniper from far away and get rewarded really good for it.
Also, have fun trying to drive the cars and/or bikes on this game, is like driving with tires coated on ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ butter. is hard not to crash onto the first tree you find.
Weapons feel good to shoot, although it has its "realism" and it's not that easy to spray and pray onto the enemies sometimes.
Speaking of, the AI is....most of the time OKAY, but i will admit that while travelling throught the map, its ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ because the minimal glance these guys have on you, you're done. I wish i could say the same about your teammates, these ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ are almost NEVER useful other than the ''multishot'' ability that they have when you enter stealth mode onto the enemies.



Idk what else to say other than, its a good entrance for the Franchise, but since its Ubisoft you will encounter some problems as a surprise to no one.
Also, i have never tried the multiplayer mode and its probably dead either way so i don't have anything to review for it.

Great game, much better than wildlanss. I think picking this up 10 years after it dropped would still be worth it. It has a very simple, but very interesting gameplay loop. Some missions feel stupid and easy while others felt incredibly new and interesting. I loved the variety of locations due to the immense map. A very fun game if you are into Tactical Shooters 3rd Person. Although, watch out for Ubisoft servers as I also have Breakpoint (the next game in this series) and the server connectivity is always an issue with these games for Online Mode.

10-year old game that holds up pretty well. Bought on sale for $3 or $4 USD. Perfect for turning off your brain, and spending an hour or two just doing various missions. Only played the main story mode. Not much story depth, but fun lore if you wanna immerse yourself. Game bugged out in one of the final zones, making it impossible to 100%, and I couldn't be bothered by the 60-hour mark.

I think 10-20 hours in, the enemy scaling kinda just stops? Mid-game and end-game zones, it was basically just different environments.

Final thoughts: Good if you're a casual shooter player that wants a little bit of tactical sandbox fun. Buy it on sale, and enjoy yourself.

I have had lots of fun playing this game, it is an excellent TPS, with fun mechanics and missions. However, because I recently upgraded my hardware to a Intel 9 285, I can no longer launch the game without waiting for a long period of time, or disabling hyper threading in my BIOS. Since I don't wish to handicap my CPU, nor wait 20 minutes, I cannot in any seriousness recommend this game. If Ubisoft fixes this issue, and doesn't treat their old titles like abandonware, I'll change my review to reflect.

On a more serious note, The game is so incredibly bad that it makes me wanna throw up in a bucket and eat from said bucket rather than play this slop for another minute. The enemy ai is completley oblivious to you unless you're hugging them. You're ai teammates are invisible to the enemy and doesn't really do anything other than break your 'immersion' and potentially revive you if get downed. 

The difficulties doesn't really seem to change anything other than how much damage you and your enemies can take. Also the game of course adheres to ubisofts classic over cooked open world formula with the only thing sticking out with this one, is that there aren't any big towers you have to climb to unlock a new part of the map. Which is sad because the game would probably have been somewhat enjoyable if it was just a linear mission based game, but instead you'll spend most of your time roaming this really uninspired and very forgettable landscape getting from mission to mission which are generally really short and also quite forgettable. 

Then there is the customization which is... mid for lack of better words. its not bad persay but it also got ubisoft written all over it with lootboxes and whack pay walls.

The game is probably kind of fun if you're playing with four players, but otherwise its so incredibly bad that I felt a need to vent it out by writing a review like a nerd

[h1]PROs:[/h1]
✅ Large weapon arsenal
✅ Weapon customization
✅ Jolly co-op on lower difficulties
✅ Stealth is mostly fun (though it becomes too reliant on it in the end-game)
✅ Fun shooter as long as you avoid Tier 1 Extreme stages
✅ Diverse scenic world with dynamic weather and day/night cycle
✅ Rebel assist mechanics
✅ Solid sound design
✅ Plenty of gadgets
✅ Scouting-to-action preplanning is genuinely fun
✅ Lots of great comical moments
✅ Revive Drone and EMP Drone are absolute game-savers on ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ difficulties
[h1]CONs:[/h1]
❌ Lot of multiplayer issues (sync problems, missions bugging out, etc.)
❌ Insane amount of enemy AI ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ on Tier 1 Extreme (instant reactions, pixel-perfect aim, tankiness, spawning, ally NPCs killing mission targets, detection, matrix dodging)
❌ Vehicle handling is abysmal (though the physics can be funny)
❌ Unreal number of places where you get stuck or fall through the world
❌ Can’t reliably use drones from choppers (character throws them into the blades)
❌ No material penetration consistency
❌ Parachute deployment is wildly inconsistent
❌ Blurry visual fidelity unless you use 1.5x+ resolution scaling
❌ Noise makers and baits are very inconsistent
❌ Boring, stereotypical American corporate/drug cartel story
❌ Flying unit seating inconsistency
❌ Object hitboxes are all over the place
❔ Certain generation Intel CPUs won’t load the game with hyperthreading enabled
[h1]Comments:[/h1]
Long story short: the game is genuinely fun to play with friends [b]as long as you don’t touch Tier 1 Extreme difficulty.[/b] (Which sucks if you’re a completionist who wants to unlock everything.)

The world looks nice, is fun to explore and travel through, and the weapon arsenal is large, giving you plenty of options to find your favourite playstyle. Weapons can also be heavily customized (although in Tier 1 Extreme mode it’s not as relevant since you can upgrade damage/stats anyway).

Gunplay and stealth are fairly enjoyable up to mid-game when you’re not forced into Tier 1 Extreme. It feels like a semi-realistic tactical shooter that unfortunately goes in the wrong direction once the difficulty spikes because of all the jank and AI issues.
I really liked the sound design (weather, terrain, guns, etc.) and the rebel assist system for scouting and distractions. Drones are extremely useful too — especially on higher difficulties. Marking enemies, preplanning, and sabotaging convoys feels great. On Tier 1 Extreme the revive drones basically became mandatory just to survive the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ enemy AI.

Now the problems.
[b]Co-op has some annoying issues:[/b]
    • Anti-air (SAM sites) and helicopter missiles aren’t synced between players — each person sees projectiles flying differently, so dodging becomes pure luck. 
    • Missions bug out constantly. We had to restart lobbies or redo missions multiple times because progress wasn’t the same for everyone. 

[b]Enemy AI on Tier 1 Extreme[/b] is the main reason the game became miserable for us (and since a lot of unlocks are gated behind it, you can’t really avoid it).
    • Split-second reactions even from enemies who shouldn’t know you’re there yet. 
    • Pixel-perfect aimbot shooting through the tiniest gaps. 
    • You need around 5-9 body shots to down one enemy or 2 headshots (unless using .50cal which one-shots anywhere), while they one- or two-shot you from any distance or angle with any gun. 
    • Instant 1-frame reactions the moment you try to knock someone out or shoot. Still haunts me that I was about to execute a sitting enemy, but he just teleport-turned around and killed me once I reached behind him. 
    • Once combat starts they instantly know your entire squad’s position, even if your teammates haven’t been spotted. 
    • Matrix dodging — they flail and dodge bullets like they have spider-sense, especially when you line up headshots. Which ruined many stealth runs. 
There are also tons of consistency issues:
    • Material penetration is odd — you can shoot through most wooden materials (but not wooden pallets). You can shoot through various thin metals, but not some. And you can also shoot through multiple meters thick oil pumps because why not. 
    • Object hitboxes. Again… some things have great hitboxes whilst some objects have 5x bigger hitboxes than the object itself. (I.E. trees.) Which again ruins tactical stealth approach. See a direct line of sight on an enemy? Nope, tree leaves apparently have a 5m hitbox radius. Shoot a sniper in the tower? Nope, the railing is apparently 5m tall/thick. 
    • Vehicle seating makes no sense (big helicopters only seat 4, small ones seat 6, same with trucks and cars). 
    • Parachute deployment is completely unreliable — you can free-fall for 4-5 seconds from some tall cliff and the game still won’t allow you to deploy parachute. Whilst sometimes you jump off a much lower and cramped height and it lets you deploy. 
Vehicle handling feels very floaty and the speeds are odd. Manageable, but not enjoyable.
The story is the typical stereotypical American corporate/drug cartel drama — nothing special.

Visuals are decent stylistically but suffer from blurriness unless you crank resolution scaling to 1.5x or higher.

Moral of the story: It’s a fun tactical shooter with great stealth, scouting, and weapon variety in a beautiful world. Just cap the difficulty at Hard and enjoy it that way. Tier 1 Extreme is not worth the sanity loss for a few cosmetics and special weapons.

We ruined our own experience by stubbornly sticking to Tier 1 the whole way. Solo is actually easier as the squad AI are the same cheating ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ as the enemies.
Probably forgot a few things, but this review is already long enough.

I went in to the game knowing it was nearly a decade old at this point but wow, I didn't expect it to be THAT bad. Ubislop of the highest order.

TL;DR: Janky, uninspired, derivative, formulaic, arbitrary. Stay away.

Gunplay is mediocre with the weird 3rd person-1st person hybrid crap, and bullets suffering 10x earth gravity. 

Travel and traversal is tedious in the sense that the world will always fight against you with janky hitboxes for climbing stuff, hilariously inconsistent fall damage, bad pathing for allies and enemies alike, and gods forbid you want to fly because the enemy is sporting respawning surface-to-air missiles, so best leave that chopper on the ground buddy.

Enemy AI is almost as bad as your companion AI. Holy moly the movements of your "team" do not make sense most of the time. As long as you don't order them to do anything they remain hidden. The second you tell them to get to your position because you are about to have a forced fight and need them /inside/ the enemy camp instead of on the road outside, they'll get spotted by any enemy immediately. And don't even get me started on the enemies that instantly respawn in the same place after being dispatched from stealth.

Stealth is so bad. Think of the bare minimum stealth in a video game, and you'll be thinking of Wildlands. It starts out with high difficulty, and once you have some levels and upgrades, it becomes laughably easy. To the point where you can clear an entire LARGE enemy encampment by just crouchwalking with a silenced pistol, and then think to yourself five minutes later "wow, was that it?".

Progression is awful. In true Ubisoft fashion, they made a gameplay loop, split the map in about a dozen different zones, and have you play that same loop a dozen times on repeat, without any variation whatsoever.

The game is pretty to look at but it is just not worth it. I'm not even sure it would have been worth it on release.

Give your money to a publisher-developer who actually cares instead.
